title: Model CoralNet, InceptionV3, dan MobileNetV2 untuk Klasifikasi Kondisi Terumbu Karang
journal: JITET (Jurnal Informatika dan Teknik Elektro Terapan)
authors: Ulfa Hidayati, Felix Windriyareksa Hardyan, Faizah Rizki Auliawati, Ali Akbar Rafsanjani, Fanka Arie Reza, Mario Mora Siregar
summary: Comparative study of CoralNet, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2 for classifying coral reef conditions into healthy, bleached, and dead categories. MobileNetV2 achieved the best performance with 97% training accuracy and 89% test accuracy.
abstract: Indonesia hosts the highest marine biodiversity in the world, with approximately 569 species and 83 genus of stony corals, accounting for 69% of global coral species and 76% of genera. Coral reefs are highly vulnerable to climate change, as environmental factors can damage photosystem II, causing corals to lose their symbiotic algae and resulting in coral bleaching. The application of Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Bag of Features (BoF) models has been explored as AI-based solutions for classifying coral reef conditions. However, these approaches rely on manual feature extraction and involve a more complex workflow compared to end-to-end deep learning methods such as CoralNet, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2. This study aims to analyze and compare the performance of these three models in classifying coral conditions into three categories: healthy, bleached, and dead.
methodology: Compared CoralNet, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2 against traditional SVM and Bag of Features approaches.
Classified coral conditions into three categories: healthy, bleached, and dead.
Evaluated end-to-end deep learning as a replacement for manual feature extraction workflows.
keyFindings: MobileNetV2 achieved the best performance with 97% training accuracy, 88% validation accuracy, and 89% test accuracy for coral reef classification.
